commands.review_summary:
Adds a new `mobile/` Expo SDK 56 TypeScript app with a React Native WebView shell for Crabbox portal/coordinator URLs, branded assets, package lock, and mobile README.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a user bug report, but the blocking review finding is source-reproducible: `mobile/App.tsx` accepts `http:` coordinators while enabling shared WebView cookies.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Missing real iOS proof plus product/security-boundary decisions make this human-review-only; an automated repair cannot prove the contributor's simulator/device flow.

target.title: [codex] add iOS mobile app
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a new `mobile/` Expo app for Crabbox with an iOS-first native shell around `crabbox.sh` and self-hosted Crabbox coordinators.

## What changed

- Scaffolds an Expo SDK 56 TypeScript app under `mobile/`.
- Builds a native WebView client that preserves portal/GitHub OAuth cookies, supports iOS back gestures and pull-to-refresh, and exposes native back, home, reload, share, open-in-browser, and coordinator settings controls.
- Persists a configurable coordinator URL, defaulting to `https://crabbox.sh`.
- Adds branded Crabbox icon/splash assets generated from a tracked SVG source.
- Documents Mac/iOS run steps, Xcode setup, Expo dev menu usage, and native prebuild flow.

## User impact

This gives Crabbox a concrete first mobile app surface focused on iOS. Users can launch the Crabbox web portal in a native container, stay signed in through WebView cookies, and switch to a team/self-hosted broker without changing code.

## Validation

- `npm run check` from `mobile/`
- `npx expo export --platform ios --output-dir /tmp/crabbox-mobile-export` from `mobile/`
- `npm audit --omit=dev` reports a moderate transitive `uuid` advisory through Expo CLI/config tooling; npm's forced fix would move Expo to an incompatible old version, so the SDK-compatible lockfile is left unchanged.

## Local iOS note

I could not launch the simulator on this machine because `xcode-select -p` points to `/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools` and `xcrun simctl` is unavailable. The README includes the Xcode selection commands needed before `npm run ios` can open the simulator.
target.author: zozo123
